note:
5月25日，乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)在明尼阿波利斯被捕时死亡一周后，美国各地的抗议活动仍在继续，那些反对“警察暴行”的人谴责全国各地不断升级的暴力事件是“机会主义的”。周日发生了“连续第五次骚乱和抢劫，导致了另一波逮捕行动”。在其他高端零售商中，苹果已经看到了自己相当一部分的攻击，现在已经采取行动保护员工，防止进一步的损害。
随着抗议活动的升级，苹果首席执行官蒂姆·库克向他的员工发出了一条信息，他说：“我们国家的灵魂和数百万人的心中都深深地刻着一种痛苦。要站在一起，我们必须为彼此挺身而出，认识到乔治·弗洛伊德被毫无意义的杀害和更长的种族主义历史正确地挑起的恐惧、伤害和愤怒。“。
库克接着说：“在苹果，我们的使命一直是，也将永远是创造技术，让人们能够让世界变得更好。我们一直从我们的多样性中汲取力量，欢迎各行各业的人光临我们在世界各地的门店，并努力打造一个包容所有人的苹果。“。
这些话是在这家科技巨头为了员工和客户的安全而决定关闭其在美国的大部分门店时被消化的，这些门店在新冠肺炎关闭后才刚刚重新开张。考虑到可能提供的战利品，苹果不出所料地成为抢劫者青睐的目标，并决定从车间和百叶窗位置清空库存。
在华盛顿特区、洛杉矶、旧金山、纽约和费城，苹果商店遭到袭击或损坏，抢劫者偷走了当时可以买到的任何产品。但这是苹果公司，任何人从那些零售店偷一部盒装iPhone都是令人头疼的。
人们早就知道，苹果公司运行着某种形式的接近软件，当设备被非法从商店拿走时，它就会禁用设备。然而，到目前为止，人们对这项技术的实际应用知之甚少。多亏了社交媒体，我们现在可以看到迎接抢劫者打开他们的新设备的消息：“这个设备已经被禁用，正在被跟踪，”它说。“将向地方当局发出警报。”
这些设备都不太可能按要求退回商店，但这不是重点。它们可能会被剥离成零件。更有可能的是，由于担心有某种形式的跟踪到位，他们会很快被抛弃。毕竟这是苹果。
除了抢劫，高端手机被盗-以iPhone居首-多年来一直是大城市的一个问题。苹果已经一步一步地试图使其设备一旦被使用就变得一文不值，包括粘性的“查找我的”功能，该功能使用户能够追踪到iPhone。“如果你的设备丢失了，”苹果公司说，“将它置于丢失模式，以便立即锁定它，并开始跟踪它的位置。”
对于那些确实丢失了iPhone的用户，苹果提供了一些有用的提示，告诉他们下一步该怎么做。但对于那些考虑从其商店偷一件的人来说，建议要简单得多--不要这样做。这不会奏效，而且可能会导致不受欢迎的敲门声。
note_en:
As protests continue across the U.S., a week on from   the death of George Floyd  while under arrest in Minneapolis on May 25, those campaigning against “  police brutality ” have condemned as “  opportunistic ” the violence that has escalated across the country. Sunday saw “  a fifth straight of rioting and looting, resulting in another wave of arrests .” Apple, among other high-end retailers, has seen its fair share of attacks and has now taken action to protect staff and prevent further damage.
  Apple CEO Tim Cook   sent a message to his employees  as those protests escalated, saying that “there is a pain deeply etched in the soul of our nation and in the hearts of millions. To stand together, we must stand up for one another, and recognize the fear, hurt, and outrage rightly provoked by the senseless killing of George Floyd and a much longer history of racism.”
  Cook went on to say that “at Apple, our mission has and always will be to create technology that empowers people to change the world for the better. We’ve always drawn strength from our diversity, welcomed people from every walk of life to our stores around the world, and strived to build an Apple that is inclusive of everyone.”
     These words were being digested as the tech giant made the decision to close the majority of its U.S. stores for the safety of those staff and its customers, stores that had only just reopened after the COVID-19 shutdown. Apple has unsurprisingly become a favored target of looters, given the likely spoils on offer, and the decision was taken to remove stock from shop floors and shutter locations.
      Apple stores were attacked or damaged in Washington D.C., Los Angeles, San Francisco, New York and Philadelphia, with looters stealing whatever products were accessible at the time. But this being Apple, there is a sting in the tail for anyone stealing a boxed iPhone from one of those retail stores.
    It has long been known that Apple operates some form of proximity software that disables a device when it is taken illegally from a store. Until now, though, little had been seen of that technology in action. Well, thanks to social media, we can now see the message that greets a looter powering up their new device: “This device has been disabled and is being tracked,” it says. “Local authorities will be alerted.”
    It is unlikely any of those devices will be returned to stores as requested, but that’s not the point. They may be stripped for parts. More likely, they will be quickly dumped over fears there is some form of tracking in place. This is Apple after all.
  Looting apart, the theft of high-end phones—with iPhones top of the list—has been an issue in major cities for years. Apple has taken step after step to try to render its devices worthless once taken, including the sticky “Find My” functionality that enables users to track down an iPhone. “If your device goes missing,” Apple says “put it in Lost Mode to lock it immediately and start tracking its location.”
  For users that do lose their iPhones, Apple provides  useful tips as to what to do next. But for those considering stealing one from its stores, the advice is much more simple—don't. It will not work and may lead to an unwelcome knock on the door.
title: 苹果用偷来的iPhone警告抢劫者：你正被跟踪
title_en: Apple Warns Looters with Stolen iPhones: You Are Being Tracked

 Apple's  system status page was not initially displaying an error when the problems started, but has been updated to confirm an issue with ‌iCloud‌ account sign ins. The support site says that some users may be unable to sign in to their ‌iCloud‌ accounts and may also be unable to access ‌iCloud‌ web apps and ‌iCloud‌ Mail.
 Some users are seeing a message about needing to verify their ‌iCloud‌ accounts, and when attempting to sign in, are getting an error message that says "The application you have selected does not exist." Other iCloud-based services such as  Apple Pay are also not working properly, and app specific passwords are failing.
 There's no word on when the ‌iCloud‌ problem will be fixed, but we'll update this article when Apple addresses it.

note:
在Recode观看的周二与Facebook员工的内部视频通话中，首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)加倍强调了他上周对唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统的一篇帖子不采取行动的有争议的决定。在帖子中，特朗普提到了美国正在进行的反对种族主义和警察暴行的抗议活动，并表示，“当抢劫开始时，枪击就开始了。”
Facebook对特朗普帖子的处理-其中的措辞类似于民权时代种族隔离主义者在提到黑人抗议者时使用的语言-导致Facebook的员工产生分歧，促使他们以前所未有的方式公开批评扎克伯格。周一，大约400名员工举行了一场虚拟的罢工，至少有两名员工辞职以示抗议，其他人威胁要辞职，几名高级经理公开反对扎克伯格的立场-呼吁他下台或以其他方式缓和特朗普的职位，就像Facebook的竞争对手推特(Twitter)已经做的那样。
这种紧张情绪蔓延到了周二的问答会议上，约有2.5万名员工收看了这场会议，几名员工提出了一些问题，高度批评了公司的行动和政策，并仔细审查了公司高层是否听取了不同种族的声音。
扎克伯格在电话会议上说：“我知道这件事的风险很高，也知道如果我们决定不去管它，很多人都会不高兴。”他接着说，在评估了特朗普声明的含义后，他决定，“就我们目前的情况而言，正确的行动是把这件事放在一边。”
扎克伯格说，他彻底分析了围绕特朗普帖子中明显提到的内容的历史，他称这是“令人不安的”，但最终没有发现这是在Facebook的政策下煽动暴力。
扎克伯格在电话会议上说：“我们基本上是在研究之后得出的结论，在我读过所有东西之后，我采访过的所有不同的人都知道，这显然是指咄咄逼人的警察-可能是过度的警察-但它没有被解读为义务警员支持者将正义掌握在自己手中的狗哨子的历史。”他还表示，总体而言，Facebook仍保留温和特朗普的权利。
“这不是允许(特朗普)说任何他想说的话，也不是我们让政府官员或决策者说他们想说的任何话。”
Facebook在很大程度上避免了缓和特朗普在其平台上的帖子。然而，今年3月，在Recode和其他媒体报道了欺骗性广告后，Facebook从其平台上删除了这些广告，这些广告使特朗普竞选团队的调查问卷似乎是2020年的官方人口普查。
在电话会议开始后，扎克伯格继续回答预先选择的名单中的问题，员工通过视频会议提问。在几次紧张的交锋中，一名员工要求扎克伯格确认扎克伯格最终决定不撤下特朗普的职位涉及多少黑人。扎克伯格的答案是：只有一个人(Facebook的全球多样性官玛克辛·威廉姆斯)。扎克伯格表示，只有大约6人参与了决策过程，其中包括Facebook首席运营官谢丽尔·桑德伯格(Sheryl Sandberg)和政策副总裁乔尔·卡普兰(Joel Kaplan)。据报道，卡普兰因阻碍该平台减少两极分化的努力，并在有争议的参议院听证会上公开支持最高法院大法官布雷特·卡瓦诺(Brett Kavanaugh)而受到密切关注。
这名员工追问，为什么Facebook的诚信主管盖伊·罗森(Guy Rosen)不在最后一批决策者之列。罗森的任务是监督围绕该平台一般用户安全的努力。
对此，扎克伯格的推理似乎有些磕磕绊绊，他先是说他不确定罗森是否是最终决定的一部分。然后他说他不是，但最终罗森负责制定和执行总体政策，而不是这个特定的决定。
这名员工在视频会议上表示：“我认为，我们不太清楚廉洁副总裁是否包括在选民压制和社会暴力问题上，这并不是一件很好的事情。”
“如果你们让我们看到缺乏透明度，我们怎么能相信Facebook的领导层呢？”另一名员工问道。
当被问及Facebook在会议中面临的批评时，该公司的一位发言人向Recode发送了以下声明：
“公开和诚实的讨论一直是Facebook文化的一部分。马克今天与员工进行了一次公开讨论，这是他多年来经常进行的。他对他们的反馈表示感谢。“。
在承认员工对这种情况的愤怒时，扎克伯格在电话会议上概述了Facebook自我指定的几个方面的改进，包括在缓和有争议的帖子的决策过程中更加透明。
最值得注意的是，扎克伯格表示，该公司正在考虑为世界各国领导人发布的煽动暴力的帖子添加标签，而不是简单地让它们张贴或删除。他还表示，由于美国可能正在进入“长期的内乱时期”，他们可能会改变他们的政策，即政府领导人可以对国家暴力做出什么样的宣布，比如过度使用警察力量。
虽然扎克伯格有时是和解的，但他的语气是为Facebook的立场辩护，即不对人们可能觉得冒犯个人的内容做出他认为是下意识的决定。他表示，即使他们真的改变政策，缓和潜在的暴力政治言论，比如特朗普的帖子，这也不会在一夜之间发生。
扎克伯格说：“必须制定这些政策。”“我们不可能在飞行中做这样的事情。”
这就提出了一个问题，为什么Facebook没有做好更多准备来缓和政治言论，因为这些言论突破了其平台关于煽动和美化暴力的规则的界限。自2016年美国总统大选以来，Facebook如何审核内容一直受到抨击，该公司承诺会做得更好。其期待已久的独立监督委员会旨在审查像特朗普的帖子这样有争议的案件，但仍未正式启动；与此同时，距离下一届美国总统选举还有不到六个月的时间。
在电话会议上，扎克伯格承认，这只是员工讨论公司如何处理围绕种族、政治和警察暴力的非常真实的争议的开始。
扎克伯格说：“我知道我们将继续讨论这个问题，一些问题是深刻的，它们不会很快消失。”
在Vox的每一天，我们都致力于回答您最重要的问题，并为您和世界各地的观众提供有能力拯救生命的信息。此时此刻，我们的使命从未像现在这样重要：通过理解来增强你们的能力。VOX的工作正在接触到比以往任何时候都多的人，但我们独特的解释性新闻品牌需要资源-特别是在大流行和经济低迷时期。您的财政贡献不会构成捐赠，但它将使我们的工作人员能够继续以当前所需的质量和音量提供免费的文章、视频和播客。请考虑今天就为VOX做出贡献。
note_en:
In an internal video call with Facebook employees on Tuesday viewed by Recode, CEO Mark Zuckerberg doubled down on his controversial decision to take no action on a post last week from President Donald Trump. In the post, Trump referred to the ongoing protests in the US against racism and police brutality and said, “when the looting starts, the shooting starts.”
 Facebook’s handling of Trump’s post — which included  language similar to what segregationists used when referring to black protesters in the civil rights era — has divided employees at Facebook and prompted them to  openly criticize Zuckerberg in a way they never have before.  Around 400 employees staged a virtual walkout of work on Monday, at  least two employees have resigned in protest, others  have threatened to resign, and  several senior-level managers have publicly disagreed with Zuckerberg’s stance — calling for him to take down or otherwise moderate Trump’s post,  as Facebook’s competitor Twitter already has.
 This tension spilled over into the Tuesday Q&A meeting that around 25,000 employees tuned into — with several employees’ posing questions that were highly critical of the company’s actions and policies, and scrutinized whether the company is listening to racially diverse voices in its upper ranks.
 “I knew that the stakes were very high on this, and knew a lot of people would be upset if we made the decision to leave it up,” Zuckerberg said on the call. He went on to say that after reviewing the implications of Trump’s statement, he decided that “the right action for where we are right now is to leave this up.”
 Zuckerberg said that he did a thorough analysis of the history around the apparent reference in Trump’s post, which he called “troubling,” but ultimately did not find it to be an incitement of violence under Facebook’s policies.
 “We basically concluded after the research and after everything I’ve read and all the different folks that I’ve talked to that the reference is clearly to aggressive policing — maybe  excessive policing — but it has no history of being read as a dog whistle for vigilante supporters to take justice into their own hands,” Zuckerberg said on the call. He also said that, overall, Facebook still reserves the right to moderate Trump.
 “This isn’t a case where [Trump] is allowed to say anything he wants, or that we let government officials or policy makers say anything they want.”
 Facebook has largely avoided moderating Trump’s posts on its platform. In March, however, after  Recode and other outlets reported on deceptive advertisements that made a Trump campaign questionnaire appear to be the official 2020 census, Facebook removed these ads from its platform.
 After opening the call, Zuckerberg went on to take questions from a preselected list, with employees asking questions via videoconference. In one of several tense exchanges, an employee asked Zuckerberg to confirm how many black people were involved in Zuckerberg’s final decision not to take down Trump’s post. Zuckerberg’s answer: just one person (Facebook’s global diversity officer, Maxine Williams). Zuckerberg said only about six people were involved in the decision-making process, including Facebook COO Sheryl Sandberg and policy VP Joel Kaplan, who has come under scrutiny for reportedly  stymieing efforts to reduce polarization on the platform and  openly supporting Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh during his controversial Senate hearings.
 The employee pressed why Facebook’s head of integrity, Guy Rosen, who is tasked with overseeing efforts around general user safety on the platform, wasn’t in the final group of decision-makers.
 In response, Zuckerberg appeared to stumble with his reasoning, first saying that he wasn’t sure if Rosen was a part of the final decision. He then saying that he wasn’t, but that ultimately Rosen is responsible for building and enforcing policies overall and not this particular decision.
 “I don’t think it’s great that we’re not super clear on whether the VP of integrity was included on a matter of voter suppression and societal violence,” the employee said on the videoconference.
 “How can we trust Facebook leadership if you show us a lack of transparency?” asked another employee.
 When asked about the criticism Facebook faced in the meeting, a spokesperson for the company sent Recode the following statement:
 “Open and honest discussion has always been a part of Facebook’s culture. Mark had an open discussion with employees today, as he has regularly over the years. He’s grateful for their feedback.”
 In an acknowledgement of employees’ anger over the situation, Zuckerberg outlined several areas of self-designated improvement for Facebook on the call, including being more transparent around the decision-making process for moderating contentious posts.
 Most notably, Zuckerberg said the company is considering adding labels to posts by world leaders that incite violence, instead of simply leaving them up or taking them down. He also said that since the US may be entering a “prolonged period of civil unrest,” they may change their policy on what kind of announcements government leaders can make about state violence, such as excessive use of police force.
 While Zuckerberg was at times conciliatory, his tone was defensive of Facebook’s stance not to make what he views as knee-jerk decisions against content that people could find personally offensive. He said even if they do change their policies around moderating potentially violent political speech like Trump’s post, it would not happen overnight.
 “These policies have to be developed,” Zuckerberg said. There’s “no way we can do something like that on the fly.”
 That brings up the question of why Facebook isn’t more prepared to moderate political speech that pushes the boundaries of its platform’s rules on inciting and glorifying violence. Since the 2016 US presidential election, how Facebook moderates content has come under fire, and the company  has promised to do better. Its long-awaited independent oversight board meant to review controversial cases like Trump’s post still hasn’t officially launched; meanwhile, the next US presidential election is less than six months away.
 On the call, Zuckerberg acknowledged that this is only the beginning of employee discussion on the company’s handling of the very real controversies coming its way around race, politics, and police violence.
 “I know we’re going to keep talking about this, some of the issues, they’re deep and they’re not going to go away any time soon,” Zuckerberg said.

与Facebook战斗是我的爱好、工作和生活。我在Telegram工作，隶属于VK(俄罗斯FB)，创建了几家社交初创公司(主要是信使)，我了解到，只有这样才能退出任何真正会破坏它的社交产品-在你的朋友和同事网络中不断推广替代平台。
坦率地说，每个人都认为要“用钱包投票”，你只需删除一个账户，但实际上这等同于不投票-不好的人只会从中受益。您必须移动您的网络。如果你和你的朋友开始搬到某个地方，你会创造一种趋势，很快就会扼杀Facebook。这是他们最大的噩梦，这就是为什么他们购买所有顶级应用程序，试图扼杀他们买不到的应用程序，等等。
所有的衡量标准都表明，要让新的社交产品为你工作，你必须达到非常低的门槛：只有几个你的亲密朋友在那里。就是这样--只需要3-5个亲密的朋友和同事，它就会永远为你服务。这一小部分朋友实际上创造了一种趋势，也将把你的大部分社交网络带到那里。这也是作为一家公司的社交产品所必须达到的--让他们的用户邀请的朋友很少。
当然，拥有这样的趋势将有助于一家公司筹集更多资金，加快营销速度，接触到越来越多的人，最终FB将成为Myspace。
这一切都是从你和你的一些朋友开始的。找个舒适的地方，搬到那里去。你会喜欢的，在过去的十年里我已经看过很多次了。
note_en:
Fighting Facebook is my hobby, work and life. I was working at Telegram, was affiliated with VK (russian FB), created several social startups (mostly messengers) and i learned that there is the only way to quit any social product that can really damage it—  constantly promoting alternative platforms within your network of friends and coworkers.
 Frankly everyone think that to “vote with a wallet” you have to just delete an account, but in reality it is an equivalent of not voting —bad actors will only benefit of this. You have to  move your network. If you and your friends start to move somewhere you  will create a trend that will kill facebook quite fast. This is their biggest nightmare, that’s why they are buying all top apps, trying to kill the one that they could not get, etc.
 All metrics shows that to make new social product work for you you have to reach very low bar: just have few your close friends there. That’s it — just 3–5 close friends and coworkers and it will work for you forever. This small amount of friends actually creates a  trend that will bring most of your network there too. This is what a social product as a company have to reach too — to make their users invite few friends.
 Of course having such trend will help a company to raise more money, speed up marketing and reach more and more people and eventually FB will became Myspace.
 All this starts from you and some of your friends. Find a cozy place and move there. You will like it, i have seen this many times in last decade.
title: 退出Facebook：正确的方式

note:
世界各地都认为6月2日是无数直接行动和抗议的日子，以支持性工作者的权利运动。自近45年前成立以来，国际妓女日重新使用了一个有时带有贬义的词，为这一天的动乱和政治行动定下了基调。6月份也是国际LGBTQ+骄傲月，这是一系列旨在突出更广泛的LGBTQ+社区不同方面的博客文章中的第一篇。
2018年，许多国际妓女日的背景是提高人们对塞斯塔/福斯塔(Sesta/Fosta)的认识，这是一项糟糕的法案，后来变成了更糟糕的法案，然后在国会参众两院匆忙通过投票。性工作倡导组织警告说，该法案将会有多么危险，因为它会破坏美国联邦法典第47 U.S.C.§230条，该法案最初是作为“通信体面法”(Communications Decency Act)的一部分颁布的，因此通过迫使互联网平台审查用户来压制在线言论。它最终通过了，不幸的是，那些倡导组织提出的严峻预测被证明是正确的。
今年，许多相同的社区也发起了类似的活动，提高了人们对另一项拟议法案的认识，该法案旨在削弱第230条：赚取IT，这是我们之前写过的。
性工作者外展项目(SWOP)是一个全国性的社会正义组织网络，致力于倡导性工作者的人权。洛杉矶分会一直在提高他们社区的意识，让他们知道如何赚取IT可能威胁到加密通信的访问，这是性行业中许多人依赖的一种减少危害的工具。这一警告也被流行的安全通讯应用Signal接受，该应用引发了人们的担忧，即如果盈利IT获得通过，可能会导致他们的软件在美国市场终结。
Hacking//Hustling是一个由性工作积极分子和数据分析师组成的集体，最初是为了响应Sesta/Fosta而成立的。他们已经主办了社区讲座、数字减少伤害研讨会和直接行动抗议活动，以提高人们对这一威胁立法的认识。Hacking//Hustling的创始人丹妮尔·布朗特(Danielle Blunt)说，“拒绝使用这些技术应该被理解为一种结构性暴力。”
以结束人口贩运、促进健康和安全为口号的性工作合法化组织警告说，这项法案将再次成为一种更容易促成性工作者被捕的手段。他们描述说，如果该法案获得通过，将进一步危及已经被边缘化的社区，而对结束人口贩运没有任何有意义的影响。
第230条是保护网上言论自由的关键，所以我们在EFF上密切关注它。今年痛苦地表明，越来越多的人依靠自己的能力在网上安全地生存。维护第230条的保护将继续为边缘化社区提供实施社区自我护理和促进减少伤害所需的资源。
note_en:
June 2nd is recognized around the world as the chosen date of countless direct actions and protests in support of the sex workers' rights movement. Since its inception nearly 45 years ago, International Whores Day reclaims a sometimes derogatory word to set the tone for a day of unrest and political action. June also marks International LGBTQ+ Pride month, and this is the first in a series of blog posts that aims to highlight different facets within the broader LGBTQ+ community.
 In 2018, the backdrop for many International Whores Day actions were to raise awareness around SESTA/FOSTA,   a bad bill that turned into a worse bill and then was rushed through votes in both houses of Congress . Sex work advocacy organizations warned how dangerous that bill would be in undermining 47 U.S.C. § 230, originally enacted as part of the Communications Decency Act, and thus silencing online speech by forcing Internet platforms to censor their users. It ultimately passed, and unfortunately the grim predictions those advocacy organizations laid out   were   proven   right .
 This year, many of the same communities are striking a similar pitch with raising awareness around another proposed bill that's aimed to weaken Section 230: EARN IT, which   we've previously written about.
   The Sex Workers Outreach Project (SWOP) is a national network of social justice organizations that dedicate their efforts to advocating for the human rights of sex workers. The LA chapter has been raising awareness in their community to how   EARN IT could threaten access to encrypted communications , a tool that many in the sex industry rely on for   harm reduction . This warning was also taken up by popular secure messaging app Signal,   which raised concern  that if EARN IT were to pass, it could bring the end of their software in American markets.
 Hacking//Hustling  is a collective of sex work activists and data analysts that originally formed in response to SESTA/FOSTA. They've hosted community teach-ins, digital harm reduction workshops, and direct action protests to raise awareness about this threatening legislation. Founder of Hacking//Hustling, Danielle Blunt says “Denying access to these technologies should be understood as a form of structural violence.”
 Decriminalize Sex Work, an organization whose tag line is "End Human Trafficking, Promote Health and Safety",   warns that this bill would once again be a means to more easily facilitate the arrest of sex workers . They describe that if it passed, it would further endanger already marginalized communities without any meaningful effect toward ending human trafficking.
  Section 230 is at the crux of protecting freedom of expression online, so   we keep a close eye on it at EFF . This year has made it painfully clear that many more people are relying on their ability to safely exist online. Upholding Section 230 protections will continue to give marginalized communities the resources they need to practice communal self care and promote harm reduction.
title: 性工作者权利倡导者对赚取IT发出警告
title_en: Sex Worker Rights Advocates Raise the Alarms about Earn IT

根据BuzzFeed新闻(BuzzFeed News)获得的一份长达两页的备忘录，美国禁毒署(FDA)已被授予全面的新权力，可以“进行秘密监视”，并收集参与乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)遇害抗议活动的人们的情报。
DEA的备忘录说，弗洛伊德的死“在全国范围内引发了广泛的抗议活动，在某些情况下，其中包括暴力和抢劫”。“该国某些地区的警察机构一直在努力维持和/或恢复秩序。”备忘录要求临时行使非常权力，周日下午，司法部一名高级官员签署了这份备忘录。
周六，司法部长威廉·巴尔(William Barr)发表了一份声明。此前，一夜之间发生了广泛的、有时是暴力的抗议活动。他在没有提供证据的情况下指责“使用安提法式策略的无政府主义和极左翼极端分子”造成了骚乱。他说，FBI、DEA、美国法警和烟酒火器和爆炸物管理局将“部署支持地方执行联邦法律的努力”。
DEA受到法规的限制，只能执行与毒品有关的联邦犯罪。但在周日，前美国检察官、巴尔的密友蒂莫西·谢伊(Timothy Shea)获得了副司法部长G·布拉德利·温斯海默(G.Bradley Weinsheheimer)的批准，可以超越该机构的授权，“履行巴尔认为合适的其他执法职责”。谢伊上个月被任命为DEA代理局长。
谢伊引用抗议活动，就为什么应该给予该机构特别的自由度提出了论点。
谢伊在备忘录中写道：“为了让DEA最大限度地协助联邦执法部门应对演变为违反联邦法律的抗议活动，DEA要求指定它执行因乔治·弗洛伊德之死引发的抗议活动而犯下的任何联邦罪行。”“DEA在全国范围内请求这一授权，期限为14天。”
美国公民自由联盟(ACLU)高级律师休·汉德赛德(Hugh Handeyside)说：“禁毒执法人员不应该对抗议活动和第一修正案保护的言论进行秘密监视。”“这种监督和信息共享很可能构成对行使宪法权利寻求正义的人的不正当调查。行政部门继续朝着错误的方向一意孤行。“。
三名DEA消息人士告诉BuzzFeed新闻，他们对这份备忘录感到不安，并将其视为司法部可能滥用权力试图抹黑抗议活动和打击受保护的第一修正案活动的一个例子。
除了“秘密监视”，备忘录还表明，DEA特工将被授权与地方和州执法部门共享情报，“干预”以“保护抗议活动的参与者和观众”，并进行面谈和搜查，逮捕被指控违反联邦法律的示威者。这份备忘录还表明，DEA特工将被授权与地方和州执法部门共享情报，“干预”以“保护抗议活动的参与者和观众”，并对涉嫌违反联邦法律的抗议者进行采访和搜查。
谢伊的备忘录获得批准一天后，特朗普总统表示，他正在“调动所有可用的联邦资源--民事和军事资源--来制止骚乱和抢劫，结束破坏和纵火，并保护守法的美国人的权利，包括你们的第二修正案的权利。”
根据起义法，总统有权为国内目的部署美军。自1992年洛杉矶骚乱期间派遣军队以来，它就没有使用过。
联邦机构渗透和监视抗议团体的历史由来已久。2015年巴尔的摩抗议弗雷迪·格雷(Freddie Gray)在警方拘留期间死亡的抗议活动期间，国土安全部(Department Of Homeland Security)监控了Twitter和其他社交媒体平台，以获取有关抗议者的情报。在密苏里州弗格森，在2014年针对迈克尔·布朗(Michael Brown)被一名白人警察杀害的抗议期间，国土安全部计划让联邦官员参与抗议活动，以进行监视和收集情报。联邦调查局对2011年开始的占领华尔街抗议运动进行了广泛的监测和监视。
目前还不清楚ATF、FBI或其他联邦执法机构是否被授予了与DEA相同的权力。
note_en:
The Drug Enforcement Administration has been granted sweeping new authority to “conduct covert surveillance” and collect intelligence on people participating in protests over the murder of George Floyd, according to a  two-page memorandum obtained by BuzzFeed News.
 Floyd’s death “has spawned widespread protests across the nation, which, in some instances, have included violence and looting,” said the DEA memo. “Police agencies in certain areas of the country have struggled to maintain and/or restore order.” The memo requests the extraordinary powers on a temporary basis, and on Sunday afternoon a senior Justice Department official signed off.
 Attorney General William Barr issued a  statement Saturday following a night of widespread and at times violent protests in which he blamed, without providing evidence, “anarchistic and far left extremists, using Antifa-like tactics,” for the unrest. He said the FBI, DEA, US Marshals, and the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives would be “deployed to support local efforts to enforce federal law.”
  The DEA is limited by statute to enforcing drug related federal crimes. But on Sunday, Timothy Shea, a former US Attorney and close confidant of Barr who was named acting administrator of the DEA last month, received approval from Associate Deputy Attorney General G. Bradley Weinsheimer to go beyond the agency’s mandate “to perform other law enforcement duties” that Barr may “deem appropriate.”
 Citing the protests, Shea lays out an argument for why the agency should be granted extraordinary latitude.
 “In order for DEA to assist to the maximum extent possible in the federal law enforcement response to protests which devolve into violations of federal law, DEA requests that it be designated to enforce any federal crime committed as a result of protests over the death of George Floyd,” Shea wrote in the memo. “DEA requests this authority on a nationwide basis for a period of fourteen days.”
  “Drug enforcement agents should not be conducting covert surveillance of protests and First Amendment protected speech,” said Hugh Handeyside, a senior attorney for the ACLU. “That kind of monitoring and information sharing may well constitute unwarranted investigation of people exercising their constitutional rights to seek justice. The executive branch continues to run headlong in the wrong direction.”
 Three DEA sources told BuzzFeed News they are troubled by the memo and see it as an example of the Justice Department potentially abusing its power in an attempt to smear the protests and crack down on protected First Amendment activity.
  In addition to “covert surveillance,” the memo indicates that DEA agents would be authorized to share intelligence with local and state law enforcement authorities, to “intervene” to “protect both participants and spectators in the protests,” and to conduct interviews and searches, and arrest protesters who are alleged to have violated federal law.
 A day after Shea’s memo was approved, President Trump  said he is “mobilizing all available federal resources — civilian and military — to stop the rioting and looting, to end the destruction and arson, and to protect the rights of law-abiding Americans, including your Second Amendment rights.”
 Under the Insurrection Act, the president has the authority to deploy the US military for domestic purposes. It has not been employed  since 1992, when troops were sent in during the Los Angeles riots.
 There is a long history of federal agencies infiltrating and surveilling protest groups. During the protests in Baltimore in 2015 over the death of Freddie Gray, who died in police custody, the Department of Homeland Security  monitored Twitter and other social media platforms for "intelligence" on the protesters. In Ferguson, Missouri, during the 2014 protests over Michael’s Brown’s killing by a white police officer, DHS planned to  'plug' federal officers into protests to conduct surveillance & collect intelligence. And the FBI conducted  extensive monitoring and surveillance of the Occupy Wall Street protest movement that began in 2011.
 It’s unknown if the ATF, FBI, or other federal law enforcement agencies have been granted the same authority as the DEA.

跳到导航跳到搜索谢尔盖·康斯坦丁诺维奇·克里卡列夫(俄语：Серге́йКонстантиновичКрикалёв，音译为谢尔盖·克里卡廖夫；生于1958年8月27日)是苏联和俄罗斯的宇航员和机械工程师。作为一名杰出的火箭科学家，他是6次太空飞行的老兵，在太空时间上排名第三，仅次于根纳季·帕达尔卡和尤里·马伦琴科：总共803天9小时39分钟。[1]他因在苏联解体期间被困在和平号上而闻名，他于2007年从太空飞行中退休，目前担任Energia太空公司副总裁。
克里卡列夫出生于苏联列宁格勒(现俄罗斯圣彼得堡)。他喜欢游泳、滑雪、骑自行车、特技飞行和业余无线电操作，尤其是从太空(呼号U5MIR)。他于1975年高中毕业。1981年，他从列宁格勒机械学院(现在称为波罗的海州立技术大学)获得机械工程学位。
1981年毕业后，他加入了负责载人航天活动的俄罗斯工业组织NPO Energia。他试验航天装备，发展航天作战方法，参加地面控制作战。1985年，当Salyut 7号空间站失败时，他在救援任务小组中工作，制定了与失控空间站对接的程序，并修复了空间站的机载系统。
克里卡列夫在1985年被选为宇航员，1986年完成了他的基础训练，并一度被分配到布兰航天飞机项目。1988年初，他开始为他在和平号空间站上的第一次长时间飞行进行训练。
这次训练包括至少六次太空行走的准备，安装一个新的舱，新的载人机动装置(MMU)的第一次测试，以及第二次苏法联合科学任务。联盟TM-7于1988年11月26日发射升空，Krikalev担任飞行工程师，Aleksandr Volkov指挥官和法国宇航员Jean-Loup Chrétien担任。之前的宇航员(弗拉基米尔·蒂托夫、穆萨·马纳罗夫和瓦莱里·波利亚科夫)在和平号上又停留了25天，这是六人机组在轨道上停留的最长时间。在之前的机组人员返回地球后，克里卡列夫、波利亚科夫和沃尔科夫继续在和平号空间站上进行实验。由于下一批机组人员的抵达被推迟，他们为和平号在1989年4月27日返回地球之前准备了一段时间的无人操作。
1990年4月，Krikalev开始准备他作为第八次长期和平号任务后备机组成员的第二次飞行，其中还包括5次EVA和一周的苏日行动。1990年12月，Krikalev开始为第九次和平号任务进行训练，其中包括10架EVA的训练。联盟TM-12于1991年5月19日发射，克里卡列夫担任飞行工程师，指挥官阿纳托利·阿采巴斯基和英国宇航员海伦·沙曼。沙尔曼在一周后带着下面的机组人员返回地球，而克里卡列夫和阿尔采巴斯基则留在了和平号上。在夏季期间，他们进行了6次EVA，以执行各种实验和一些空间站维护任务。
1991年7月，克里卡列夫同意留在和平号担任下一批机组的飞行工程师，下一批机组定于10月份抵达，因为接下来的两个计划航班已经减少到一个。1991年10月2日，联盟TM-13飞行上的工程师职位由来自哈萨克斯坦苏维埃社会主义共和国的宇航员托克塔尔·奥巴基罗夫(Toktar Aubakirov)填补，他没有接受过长期任务的培训。1991年10月10日，奥巴基罗夫和第一位奥地利宇航员弗朗茨·维博克(Franz Viehböck)都与阿尔采巴斯基一起返回。指挥官亚历山大·沃尔科夫(Alexander Volkov)与克里卡列夫(Krikalev)留在船上。10月份机组人员更换后，沃尔科夫和克里卡列夫继续进行和平号实验操作，并在1992年3月25日返回地球之前进行了另一次EVA。
在和平号上的各种任务中，克里卡列夫定期与全球各地的各种业余无线电操作员(火腿)进行交流。Krikalev和业余无线电操作员玛格丽特·亚昆托之间形成了特别长的关系。在他的一次太空逗留期间，他曾在整整一年的时间里每天联系她一次。Krikalev和Iaquinto历史上第一次通过分组无线电在轨道空间站和业余无线电操作员之间成功通信。他们就个人问题和政治问题进行了交流。亚昆托设立了一个临时的数字公告牌，和平号宇航员经常使用它来获取有关苏联解体状况的未经审查的西方新闻和信息。[2]
1991年12月26日苏联解体时，克里卡列夫正在太空中。由于拜科努尔发射场和着陆区都位于新独立的哈萨克斯坦，他的任务命运存在很大不确定性。他在太空的停留时间比原计划长了两倍，总共在太空度过了311天。[3]他于3月25日返回地球，有时被称为最后一位苏联公民。[3][4][5][6]这些事件在罗马尼亚电影制作人Andrei Ujică&#39；1995年的纪录片“走出现在”中被记录下来，并与之联系在一起。[7]古巴作曲家西尔维奥·罗德里格斯(Silvio Rodríguez)创作的歌曲《卡西奥比亚》(Casiopea)虚构了克里卡列夫对此的感受。另一部受基拉列夫故事启发的虚构作品是2017年由欧内斯托·达拉纳斯执导的电影《塞尔吉奥与谢尔盖》。[8]。
1992年10月，美国国家航空航天局(NASA)宣布，一名经验丰富的宇航员将搭乘未来的航天飞机任务。克里卡列夫是俄罗斯航天局提名的与STS-60机组人员进行任务专家培训的两名候选人之一。1993年4月，他被指派为主要任务专家。1993年9月，弗拉基米尔·蒂托夫(Vladimir Titov)被选为STS-63航班，克里卡列夫(Krikalev)训练作为他的后备。
克里卡列夫乘坐STS-60飞行，这是美俄首次联合执行航天飞机任务。STS-60于1994年2月3日发射，是太空居住舱-2(Spacehab-2)的第二次飞行，也是尾流盾牌设施(WSF-1)的第一次飞行。在为期八天的飞行中，发现号的机组人员进行了各种各样的材料科学实验，既有在尾盾设施上进行的，也有在太空总署、地球观测和生命科学实验中进行的。Krikalev在飞行期间执行了远程机械手系统(RMS)的大部分操作。1994年2月11日，STS-60沿着地球130圈3439705海里(6370334公里)降落在佛罗里达州肯尼迪航天中心。随着这次飞行的完成，克里卡列夫又在太空中记录了8天7小时9分钟。
克里卡列夫在经历了美国人在STS-60上的经历后，回到了俄罗斯执行任务。他定期回到休斯顿的约翰逊航天中心，与Capcom在俄罗斯的任务控制和地面控制员合作，支持美俄联合任务。到目前为止，他已经支持STS-63、STS-71、STS-74和STS-76。
1998年12月，克里卡列夫和罗伯特·卡巴纳成为第一批进入国际空间站的人，当时他们打开了美国团结号舱的灯。
Krikalev乘坐STS-88奋进号(1998年12月4-15日)飞行，这是国际空间站的第一次组装任务。在为期12天的任务中，联合舱与扎里亚舱交配。两名机组人员进行了三次太空行走，以连接脐带，并连接工具和硬件，以供未来的EVA使用。机组人员还进行了IMAX货舱相机(ICBC)操作，并部署了两颗卫星Might Sat 1和SAC-A。这项任务在地球185圈内完成，耗时283小时18分钟。
克里卡列夫是远征1号机组人员中的一员。二零零零年十月三十一日，他们搭乘联盟号火箭从哈萨克斯坦拜科努尔发射场发射升空，并于二零零零年十一月二日与空间站成功对接。在空间站逗留期间，他们为未来的工作人员准备了轨道前哨的内部。他们还看到，随着美国太阳能电池板结构和美国命运实验室模块的安装，空间站的规模也在扩大。他们与STS-102机组人员一起离开空间站，于3月18日脱离空间站，并于2001年3月21日降落在佛罗里达州的肯尼迪航天中心。
克里卡列夫也是第11探险队的指挥官，他在国际空间站生活和工作了6个月。这是他第三次飞往国际空间站。探险队11号于2005年4月14日从哈萨克斯坦拜科努尔发射场搭乘联盟号宇宙飞船发射升空，并于2005年4月16日与国际空间站对接。经过8天的联合行动和交接简报，他们替换了乘坐联盟号返回地球的远征10号机组人员。探险队11号的计划要求进行两次太空行走，第一次是在8月份从美国探索号气闸舱穿着美国宇航服进行的，第二次是在9月份，是在俄罗斯宇航服从Pir气闸舱进行的。2005年8月16日凌晨1：44。美国东部夏令时，他打破了谢尔盖·阿夫德耶夫保持的748天的太空记录。[1]
探险队11号于2005年10月10日下午5点49分离开国际空间站。美国东部夏令时2005年10月10日晚上9点09分降落在哈萨克斯坦。美国东部夏令时。取而代之的是第12次远征的船员威廉·S·麦克阿瑟和瓦莱里·托卡列夫。
在完成他的第六次太空飞行中，Krikalev已经在太空飞行了803天9小时39分钟，其中包括8次EVA。目前，他是继根纳季·帕达尔卡(Gennady Padalka)和尤里·马伦琴科(Yuri Malenchenko)之后，在太空停留时间最多的第三名。
克里卡列夫对国际空间站的贡献并不局限于他在轨的时间。2007年6月15日，克里卡列夫本人被带到俄罗斯任务控制中心，指导第15次远征飞行工程师奥列格·科托夫和国际空间站指挥官费奥多·尤尔奇金如何启动俄罗斯部分瘫痪的计算机系统。
2007年2月15日，克里卡列夫被任命为科罗廖夫火箭航天公司Energia(俄语：Ракетно-космическаякорпорация&34；Энергия&34；им)副总裁。美国航空航天局(С.П.Королева：行情)负责载人航天飞行。在那个办公室里，他是尤里·加加林宇航员训练中心的管理员。
一个以Krikalev为基础的角色出现在古巴电影电视剧Sergio and Sergei中，在这部电视剧中，哈瓦那的一名教授和业余无线电爱好者联系了和平号空间站上一位名叫Sergei的宇航员。这部电影将当时古巴的经济困难与苏联解体相提并论，苏联解体时，现实生活中的克里卡列夫正在和平号上。[9]。
他是俄罗斯和苏联国家特技飞行队的成员，1983年是莫斯科冠军，1986年是苏联冠军。
他打破了谢尔盖·阿夫德耶夫之前在国际空间站第11次远征期间在太空停留的总时间(747.59天)的职业生涯纪录。克里卡列夫在太空中总共记录了803天9小时39分钟。
2007年5月23日，谢尔盖·克里卡列夫与指挥家瓦列里·热尔吉耶夫一起被选为圣彼得堡荣誉公民。
克里卡列夫是在2014年索契冬奥会开幕式上被选为升起俄罗斯国旗的五名宇航员之一。[10]。
这篇文章纳入了来自美国国家航空航天局网站或文件的公共领域材料。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  Sergei Konstantinovich Krikalev ( Russian:  Серге́й Константинович Крикалёв, also transliterated as  Sergei Krikalyov; born August 27, 1958) is a Soviet and Russian  cosmonaut and mechanical engineer. As a prominent  rocket scientist, he is a veteran of six space flights and ranks third to  Gennady Padalka and  Yuri Malenchenko for the amount of time in space: a total of 803 days, 9 hours, and 39 minutes.  [1] Known for being stranded on board the  Mir during the  dissolution of the Soviet Union, he retired from spaceflight in 2007 and is currently working as vice president of  Space Corporation Energia.
   Krikalev was born in  Leningrad, in the Soviet Union (now  St. Petersburg, Russia) . He enjoys swimming, skiing, cycling, aerobatic flying, and  amateur radio operations, particularly from space (callsign U5MIR). He graduated from high school in 1975. In 1981, he received a  mechanical engineering degree from the  Leningrad Mechanical Institute, now called  Baltic State Technical University.
 After graduation in 1981, he joined  NPO Energia, the Russian industrial organization responsible for manned space flight activities. He tested space flight equipment, developed space operations methods, and participated in ground control operations. When the  Salyut 7 space station failed in 1985, he worked on the rescue mission team, developing procedures for docking with the uncontrolled station and repairing the station's on-board system.
  Krikalev was selected as a  cosmonaut in 1985, completed his basic training in 1986, and, for a time, was assigned to the  Buran Shuttle program. In early 1988, he began training for his first long-duration flight aboard the  Mir space station.
 This training included preparations for at least six  EVAs (space walks), installation of a new module, the first test of the new  Manned Maneuvering Unit (MMU), and the second joint Soviet-French science mission.  Soyuz TM-7 was launched on November 26, 1988, with Krikalev as flight engineer, Commander  Aleksandr Volkov, and French astronaut  Jean-Loup Chrétien. The previous crew ( Vladimir Titov,  Musa Manarov, and  Valeri Polyakov) remained on Mir for another 25 days, marking the longest period a six-person crew had been in orbit. After the previous crew returned to Earth, Krikalev, Polyakov, and Volkov continued to conduct experiments aboard the Mir station. Because arrival of the next crew had been delayed, they prepared the Mir for a period of unmanned operations before returning to Earth on April 27, 1989.
 In April 1990, Krikalev began preparing for his second flight as a member of the backup crew for the eighth long-duration Mir mission, which also included five EVAs and a week of Soviet-Japanese operations. In December 1990, Krikalev began training for the ninth Mir mission which included training for ten EVAs.  Soyuz TM-12 launched on May 19, 1991, with Krikalev as flight engineer, Commander  Anatoly Artsebarsky, and British astronaut  Helen Sharman. Sharman returned to Earth with the following crew after one week, while Krikalev and Artsebarsky remained on Mir. During the summer, they conducted six EVAs to perform a variety of experiments and some station maintenance tasks.
 In July 1991, Krikalev agreed to stay on Mir as flight engineer for the next crew, scheduled to arrive in October because the next two planned flights had been reduced to one. The  engineer slot on the  Soyuz TM-13 flight on October 2, 1991, was filled by  Toktar Aubakirov, an astronaut from the  Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic, who had not been trained for a long-duration mission. Both Aubakirov and  Franz Viehböck, the first Austrian astronaut, returned with Artsebarsky on 10 October 1991. Commander  Alexander Volkov remained on board with Krikalev. After the crew replacement in October, Volkov and Krikalev continued Mir experiment operations and conducted another EVA before returning to Earth on March 25, 1992.
 Throughout his various missions aboard Mir, Krikalev regularly communicated with various  amateur radio operators (hams) across the globe. A particularly lengthy relationship was formed between Krikalev and amateur radio operator  Margaret Iaquinto. At one point during one of his stays in space, he contacted her once a day for an entire year. Krikalev and Iaquinto successfully communicated via  packet radio for the first time in history between an orbiting space station, and an amateur radio operator. They communicated about personal matters, as well as political ones. Iaquinto set up a makeshift digital bulletin board that the Mir cosmonauts would often use to obtain uncensored western news and information regarding the state of the collapsing Soviet Union.  [2]
 Krikalev was in space when  the Soviet Union was dissolved on December 26, 1991. With the  Baikonur Cosmodrome and the landing area both being located in the newly-independent Kazakhstan, there was a lot of uncertainty about the fate of his mission. He remained in space for two times longer than originally planned, spending a total of 311 days in space.  [3] He returned to Earth on March 25 and is sometimes referred to as the "last Soviet citizen".  [3]  [4]  [5]  [6] These events are documented and contextualized in Romanian filmmaker  Andrei Ujică's 1995  documentary   Out of the Present.  [7] A fictional account of how Krikalev may have felt about this is described in the song  Casiopea, written by  Cuban songwriter  Silvio Rodríguez. Another fictional work inspired by Kiralev story is   Sergio & Sergei, a 2017 film directed by  Ernesto Daranas.  [8]
   In October 1992, NASA announced that an experienced cosmonaut would fly aboard a future  Space Shuttle mission. Krikalev was one of two candidates named by the  Russian Space Agency for mission specialist training with the crew of  STS-60. In April 1993, he was assigned as prime mission specialist. In September 1993, Vladimir Titov was selected to fly on  STS-63 with Krikalev training as his back-up.
 Krikalev flew on STS-60, the first joint U.S./Russian Space Shuttle Mission. Launched on February 3, 1994, STS-60 was the second flight of the  Space Habitation Module-2 (Spacehab-2), and the first flight of the  Wake Shield Facility (WSF-1). During the eight-day flight, the crew of   Discovery conducted a wide variety of materials science experiments, both on the Wake Shield Facility and in the Spacehab, earth observation, and life science experiments. Krikalev conducted significant portions of the  Remote Manipulator System (RMS) operations during the flight. Following 130 orbits of the Earth in 3,439,705 nautical miles (6,370,334 km), STS-60 landed at  Kennedy Space Center,  Florida, on 11 February 1994. With the completion of this flight, Krikalev logged an additional eight days, seven hours, nine minutes in space.
 Krikalev returned to duty in Russia following his American experience on STS-60. Periodically he returned to the  Johnson Space Center in  Houston to work with  CAPCOM in  Mission Control and ground controllers in Russia supporting joint U.S./Russian Missions. To date he has supported  STS-63,  STS-71,  STS-74 and  STS-76.
 Krikalev and  Robert Cabana became the first people to enter the  ISS in December, 1998, when they turned on the lights in the US module  Unity.
  Krikalev flew on  STS-88  Endeavour (4–15 December 1998), the first International Space Station assembly mission. During the 12-day mission the Unity Module was mated with  Zarya module. Two crew members performed three space walks to connect umbilicals and attach tools and hardware for use in future EVAs. The crew also performed  IMAX Cargo Bay Camera (ICBC) operations, and deployed two satellites, Mighty Sat 1 and SAC-A. The mission was accomplished in 185 orbits of the  Earth in 283 hours and 18 minutes.
  Krikalev was a member of the  Expedition 1 crew. They launched October 31, 2000, on a Soyuz rocket from  Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan, successfully docking with the station on November 2, 2000. During their stay on the station they prepared the inside of the orbital outpost for future crews. They also saw the station grow in size with the installation of the U.S. solar array structure and the U.S.  Destiny Laboratory Module. They left the station with the  STS-102 crew, undocking from the station on 18 March with landing at the Kennedy Space Center, Florida, on 21 March 2001.
 Krikalev was also the Commander of  Expedition 11. He lived and worked aboard the  International Space Station on a six-month tour of duty. This was the third time he had flown to the  International Space Station. Expedition 11 launched from  Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan on 14 April 2005 aboard a Soyuz spacecraft and docked with the ISS on 16 April 2005. Following 8 days of joint operations and handover briefings, they replaced the  Expedition 10 crew who returned to earth aboard Soyuz. Expedition 11 plans called for two spacewalks, the first in August from the US  Quest Airlock in US spacesuits, and the second, in September, in Russian spacesuits from the  Pirs airlock. On August 16, 2005 at 1:44 a.m. EDT he passed the record of 748 days in space held by  Sergei Avdeyev.  [1]
 Expedition 11 undocked from the ISS on 10 October 2005 at 5:49 p.m. EDT and landed in Kazakhstan on 10 October 2005 at 9:09 p.m. EDT. They were replaced by  William S. McArthur and  Valeri Tokarev, the crew of  Expedition 12.  [1]
 In completing his sixth space flight, Krikalev has logged 803 days and 9 hours and 39 minutes in space, including eight EVAs. He is currently third to  Gennady Padalka and  Yuri Malenchenko in the record for the  most time spent in space.
 Krikalev's contributions to the ISS were not limited to his on-orbit time. On June 15, 2007, it was Krikalev himself who was brought into the Russian  Mission Control center to instruct  Expedition 15 Flight Engineer  Oleg Kotov on how he and ISS Commander  Fyodor Yurchikhin could jump-start the Russian segment's crippled computer systems.
  On February 15, 2007, Krikalev was appointed Vice President of the  S.P. Korolev Rocket and Space Corporation Energia (Russian: Ракетно-космическая корпорация "Энергия" им. С.П.Королева) in charge of manned space flights. In that office, he is the administrator of the  Yuri Gagarin Cosmonaut Training Center.
  A character based on Krikalev features in the Cuban film drama  Sergio and Sergei, in which a professor and amateur radio enthusiast in Havana contacts a cosmonaut named Sergei aboard the Mir space station. The film draws parallels between economic hardships in Cuba at the time and the fall of the Soviet Union, which occurred as the real-life Krikalev was aboard Mir.  [9]
    He was a member of the Russian and  Soviet national  aerobatic flying teams, and was Champion of  Moscow in 1983, and Champion of the  Soviet Union in 1986.
     He overtook  Sergei Avdeyev's previous record for the career total time spent in space (747.59 days) during  Expedition 11 to the  International Space Station. Krikalev has logged a total of 803 days and 9 hours and 39 minutes in space.
 On 23 May 2007 Sergei Krikalev was selected as an  honorary citizen of  Saint Petersburg together with conductor  Valery Gergiev.
 Krikalev was one of five cosmonauts selected to raise the Russian flag at the Sochi 2014 Winter Olympics opening ceremony.  [10]
    This article incorporates  public domain material from websites or documents of the  National Aeronautics and Space Administration.
title: 谢尔盖·克里卡列夫

note:
弗兰克·鲁奇茨卡告诉他的病理学家在第一个新冠肺炎病人去世之前做好准备。3月初，苏黎世大学医院心脏科主任鲁斯奇兹卡注意到，患有这种疾病的患者有奇怪的症状，当时被认为主要是呼吸道感染。许多患者有急性肾功能衰竭、器官损伤和神秘的血块。几周后，第一具身体被解剖：肺部的毛细血管里散落着微小的凝块和死亡细胞，炎症使供应身体每个器官的血管膨胀。
病理学家从未见过这样的情况。但研究结果向鲁斯奇兹卡表明了他的患者遭受如此多痛苦的原因：病毒以他们的血管为目标。
自从苏黎世研究小组的研究结果于4月中旬发表以来，数十项研究显示，死于新冠肺炎的人的血管损伤模式类似。例如，5月21日发表在“新英格兰医学杂志”上的一篇论文显示，新冠肺炎受害者的肺部凝块是死于H1N1流感患者的9倍。其他研究已经注意到儿童的炎症症状，以及其他健康的年轻人的中风。现在，研究人员已经将这些发现编织成一个新的假说，解释为什么一些患者在住院大约一周后陷入新冠肺炎致命的“第二阶段”。
比利时研究机构VIB的血管生物学家彼得·卡梅利特(Peter Carmeliet)解释说，关键是对血管内皮细胞的直接和间接损害，特别是对肺部血管内皮细胞的损害。他与人合著了5月21日发表在《自然评论免疫学》(Nature Reviews Immunology)上的一篇论文。通过攻击这些细胞，新冠肺炎感染导致血管渗漏和血液凝结。这些变化反过来会引发全身炎症，并助长导致大多数患者死亡的急性呼吸窘迫综合征(ARDS)。
“这是一个恶性循环，”没有参与这项新研究的宾夕法尼亚大学医院的肺部强化专家尼拉姆·曼格尔穆尔蒂(Nilam Mangalmurti)说。
这一机制可以解释为什么这种疾病会重创一些患有肥胖症、糖尿病和心血管疾病的患者：他们血管内的细胞已经受损。如果是这样的话，用于治疗这些疾病的药物可能有助于防止其他新冠肺炎患者滑入严重疾病。“(疫苗)将是非常棒的，”辛辛那提大学医学院的心脏病学家理查德·贝克尔(Richard Becker)说，他在5月15日发表在“血栓与血栓学杂志”(Journal Of Thrombosis And Thrombolytis)上的一篇综述中概述了类似的心血管级联反应。但他说，在安全、有效的疫苗出现之前，这样的疗法可能是“一个良好的开始”。
在健康人中，内皮细胞有助于调节血压、预防炎症和抑制凝血，部分是通过持续产生一氧化氮(NO)；它们也是进出血液的分子的守门人。当受伤时，它们会向免疫细胞和凝血因子发出一系列复杂的信号，这些细胞和凝血因子会迅速修复部位。他们还警告同行的内皮细胞要警惕入侵者。
根据苏黎世医院的尸检报告、疾病的流行病学，以及新型冠状病毒在实验室细胞中的表现，Carmeliet和他的同事认为，病毒可能会让系统失控。
当SARS-CoV-2进入肺部时，它会侵入气囊中的细胞，气囊将氧气转移到血液中。围绕这些囊的是毛细血管，像砖块一样排列着内皮细胞。病毒直接侵入这些细胞中的一些；其他的则被“激活”，很可能是对入侵的病毒和其他受损细胞发出的信号做出反应。一些受感染的细胞可能会自杀。“细胞就这么死了，这不是一种安静的死亡，”曼格尔穆尔蒂说。“所有的内容都泄露了。”
Carmeliet和他的同事认为，激活细胞的损伤和其他变化会引发血管渗漏，使气囊充满液体，这是ARDS的一个标志。白血球蜂拥至肺部，没有任何产量可能直线下降。免疫细胞与激活的内皮细胞一起释放大量信号分子，包括白细胞介素，这些分子会升高局部血压，削弱细胞连接。内皮细胞的损伤也暴露了它们下面的膜。
暴露的膜反过来会引发不受控制的凝血。内皮细胞和免疫细胞火上浇油，招募额外的凝血因子和血小板，帮助形成凝块。这些凝块会降解成关键的生物标志物D-二聚体，从而产生极高的水平，提醒临床医生注意有麻烦的患者(见下图)。最终，这种凝结扩散到全身，阻塞重要器官的血液供应。
正常状态严重新冠肺炎血管渗漏毛细血管1号血小板。肺内D-二聚体免疫细胞液。。。SARS-CoV-2细胞因子风暴纤维蛋白凝块2 3炎症白细胞介素。一系列损伤一种新的假说表明，SARS-CoV-2攻击了排列在肺气囊周围血管的内皮细胞，或者是肺泡。可能会造成螺旋式的破坏。受损的内皮细胞会导致液体从血管中渗出，引发血液凝结，并召集大量免疫细胞和信使分子，从而引起广泛的炎症。肺泡内皮细胞紧密连接凝固性炎症1 2 3。
这些连锁反应最终导致炎症的最终破坏性阶段。就像凝血一样，炎症也是一种重要的防御手段，它会派出一支名为细胞因子的多样化的细胞和信使分子大军，以对抗入侵者，并清除战争的残骸。但在新冠肺炎身上，这种反应在一场致命的细胞因子风暴中螺旋上升，失控，使患者的身体陷入休克。
Ruschizka说，三步假说对他在病人身上看到的情况“非常有意义”；他已经把Carmeliet的论文寄给了同事。他说，这一系列的途径也可能解释了为什么一些没有新冠肺炎已知危险因素的年轻人会患上重病：他们可能患有未经诊断的凝血或自身免疫性疾病，如类风湿性关节炎，这些疾病放大了sars-cov-2感染的影响。
Becker说，这种关于内皮细胞关键作用的新兴观点表明，一些现有的药物可能会抑制甚至阻止这种疾病致命的第二阶段。炎症和凝血在新冠肺炎发病中起作用的证据已经激发了美国和欧洲数十种抗凝、抗炎和抗血小板药物的试验。
Ruschizka认为另一种常用的处方药可能会有帮助：他汀类药物。它们通常用来降低胆固醇，还可以减少炎症，改善内皮细胞功能。
MangalMurti欢迎这样的试验，但警告说，患者的反应可能会有所不同，这取决于他们的内皮细胞开始时的健康程度。“一种尺码不可能适合所有人。”
note_en:
Frank Ruschitzka told his pathologist to be ready before the first COVID-19 patient died. In early March, Ruschitzka, who leads the cardiology department at University Hospital Zürich, noticed that patients with the disease had strange symptoms for what was then thought to be chiefly a respiratory infection. Many patients had acute kidney failure, organ damage, and mysterious blood clots. Several weeks later, the first body was autopsied: Tiny clots and dead cells littered the capillaries of the lungs, and inflammation had distended blood vessels supplying every organ in the body.
 The pathologist had never seen anything like it. But the results showed Ruschitzka why his patients were suffering so much: The virus had targeted their blood vessels.
    Since the Zürich team’s findings were published in mid-April, dozens of studies have revealed similar patterns of vascular damage in people who died of COVID-19. For example, a 21 May paper in The New England Journal of Medicine showed that the lungs of COVID-19 victims had nine times as many clots as those who died of the H1N1 flu. Other studies have noted  inflammatory symptoms in children and strokes in otherwise healthy young adults. Now, researchers have woven these findings into a new hypothesis explaining why some patients slip into a fatal “second phase” of COVID-19, 1 week or so after hospitalization.
 The key is direct and indirect damage to the endothelial cells that line the blood vessels, particularly in the lungs, explains Peter Carmeliet, a vascular biologist at the Belgian research institute VIB and co-author of a 21 May paper in  Nature Reviews Immunology. By attacking those cells, COVID-19 infection causes vessels to leak and blood to clot. Those changes in turn spark inflammation throughout the body and fuel the acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) responsible for most patient deaths.
 “It’s a vicious cycle,” says Nilam Mangalmurti, a pulmonary intensivist at the Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania, who was not involved in the new research.
 This mechanism could explain why the disease pummels some patients who have obesity, diabetes, and cardiovascular conditions: The cells lining their blood vessels are already compromised. If so, drugs used to treat these conditions might help prevent other COVID-19 patients from sliding into serious disease. “[A vaccine] would be terrific,” says Richard Becker, a cardiologist at the University of Cincinnati College of Medicine who outlined a similar cardiovascular cascade in a 15 May review in the Journal of Thrombosis and Thrombolytis. But until a safe, effective vaccine is available, he says, such therapeutics might be “a good start.”
 In healthy individuals, endothelial cells help regulate blood pressure, prevent inflammation, and inhibit clotting, in part through the continual production of nitric oxide (NO); they also serve as gatekeepers for molecules passing in and out of the bloodstream. When injured, they send out a complex array of signals to immune cells and clotting factors, which rush to repair the site. And they warn their fellow endothelial cells to be on alert for invaders.
 Based on autopsy reports like those from the Zürich hospital, the epidemiology of the disease, and how the new coronavirus behaves in cells in the lab, Carmeliet and colleagues believe the virus can send that system spinning out of control.
 When SARS-CoV-2 enters the lungs, it invades cells in the air sacs that transfer oxygen to the blood. Surrounding those sacs are capillaries lined like bricks with endothelial cells. The virus directly invades some of those cells; others become “activated,” likely in response to signals from the invading virus and other damaged cells. Some infected cells likely commit suicide. “It’s not a quiet death where the cell just dies,” Mangalmurti says. “All the contents leak out.”
 Carmeliet and colleagues suggest damage and other changes in the activated cells trigger vascular leakage, flooding the air sacs with fluid, a hallmark of ARDS. White blood cells swarm to the lungs and NO production likely plummets. Together with the activated endothelial cells, the immune cells release a host of signaling molecules, including interleukins, which raise local blood pressure and weaken cell junctions. Damage to the endothelial cells also exposes the membrane underneath them.
 That exposed membrane in turn triggers uncontrolled clotting. The endothelial and immune cells add fuel to the fire, recruiting additional clotting factors and platelets, which help form clots. Those clots degrade into the key biomarker D-dimer, creating the sky-high levels that alert clinicians to patients in trouble (see graphic, below). Eventually, such clotting spreads throughout the body and blocks the blood supply within vital organs.
  Normal state       Severe COVID-19     Vascular leakage                                                                                                                                                                                                  Capillary         1      Platelets     D-dimer        Immune  cells                                                                                              Fluid in  lungs                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   SARS-CoV-2     Cytokine storm     Fibrin clot         2          3      Inflammation     Interleukins                                                                                                                                                                                                                    A cascade of injury A new hypothesis suggests SARS-CoV-2 attacks  the endothelial cells that line the blood vessels  surrounding the lungs’ air sacs, or alveoli. A spiral  of damage can result. Injured endothelial cells  cause fluid to leak out of vessels, trigger blood  clotting, and summon a host of immune cells and  messenger molecules that cause widespread  inflammation.     Lung alveolus       Endothelial cell       Tight junctions       Clotting     Inflammation     1     2     3
  These chain reactions culminate in a final, destructive phase of inflammation. Like clotting, inflammation is an essential defense, sending a diverse army of cells and messenger molecules called cytokines to fight invaders and mop up the debris of battle. But in COVID-19, this reaction spirals out of control in a deadly cytokine storm and plunges patients’ bodies into shock.
 Ruschitzka says the three-step hypothesis “makes perfect sense” of what he saw in his patients; he’s already sending the Carmeliet paper to colleagues. He says the array of pathways may also explain why some young people without known risk factors for COVID-19 become seriously ill: They might have undiagnosed clotting or autoimmune disorders, such as rheumatoid arthritis, that amplify the effects of SARS-CoV-2 infection.
 This emerging view of the key role of endothelial cells suggests that a number of existing drugs might dampen or even arrest the fatal second phase of the disease, Becker says. Already, evidence that inflammation and clotting play a role in COVID-19 has inspired dozens of trials in the United States and Europe of anticlotting, anti-inflammatory, and antiplatelet drugs.
 Ruschitzka thinks another commonly prescribed drug might help: statins. Typically taken to lower cholesterol, they also reduce inflammation and improve endothelial cell function.
 Mangalmurti welcomes such trials but cautions that patients may respond differently depending on how healthy their endothelial cells are to start. “One size does not fit all.”
title: 血管攻击可能引发冠状病毒“致命”的“第二阶段”--科学
title_en: Blood vessel attack could trigger coronavirus’ fatal ‘second phase’ – Science

绝大多数推特和脸书员工(76%和78%)都支持推特的决定，即在特朗普最近发布的关于投票的虚假声明的推文中加入事实核查旗帜。至少根据面向公司员工的匿名即时通讯应用程序Blind的开发团队在上周末发起的一项匿名调查，这至少是这样。
这一数据发布之际，针对特朗普在社交媒体上的行为的争议达到了白热化，此前，全国性的抗议活动、特朗普在Twitter上发出的暴力威胁(后来被该公司隐藏)以及Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)拒绝从他自己的社交网络上删除或标记特朗普的煽动性帖子。(这导致Facebook的一些员工举行了虚拟罢工，或者公开辞职，并发表了针对扎克伯格的辞职声明。)。
这项盲目调查中的另一个问题可能会揭示Facebook内部的紧张局势：略高于52%的Facebook员工不同意社交网络不应该对政治言论进行事实核查的观点。推特员工持不同意见的比例为57%，其中一半的人属于有点不同意的类别。
盲人调查的对象是主要科技和金融服务公司的1500多名员工，其中亚马逊、谷歌、微软和Facebook的员工代表最多。虽然数据样本很小，但有几件事值得注意：在事实核查特朗普的推文方面，Twitter和Facebook员工在这项调查中的观点与其他同事的观点相当接近--同时，社交网络员工总体上更有可能支持事实核查政治声明。
与去年10月的这项盲目调查进行比较和对比：大多数Facebook员工认为，允许虚假声明的政治广告的政策可能会改变2020年的选举。
note_en:
An overwhelming number of both Twitter and Facebook employees (76% and 78%) support Twitter's decision to add a fact check flag on Trump's  recent tweet making false claims about voting. That's at least according to an anonymous survey launched last weekend by the  development team of  Blind, an anonymous messaging app for company staffers.
  This data comes as controversy over Trump's behavior on social media has reached a fever pitch in the wake of nation-wide protests, a Trump threat of violence on Twitter (subsequently hidden by the company), and Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg's refusal to remove or flag Trump's inflammatory posts from his own social network. (Which has caused some Facebook employees to stage virtual walk-outs or  publicly quit with a resignation statement against Zuckerberg.)
  Another question in this Blind survey may shed light on this roiling tension within Facebook:       By a slight majority of 52%, Facebook employees disagreed with the notion that social networks shouldn't fact check political speech. Twitter employees disagreed at a rate of 57%, with half as many falling into the "somewhat disagree" category.
  Blind's survey was taken by just over 1500 employees of major tech and financial services companies, with staffers at Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Facebook being the most represented.  While the data sample is small, a couple things stand out: Twitter and Facebook employee's opinions in this survey are quite close to those among other colleagues in terms of fact checking Trump tweets -- while at the same time, the social network employees are much  more likely to support fact checking political statements in general.
    Compare and contrast with this Blind survey from last October:  Majority Of Facebook Employees Believe Policy Allowing Political Ads With False Claims May Change 2020 Election.
title: Facebook和Twitter员工强烈支持事实核查特朗普-调查
title_en: Facebook and Twitter employees strongly support fact-checking Trump – survey

note:
周一，一群美国参议员提出了一项监管联系人追踪应用程序的法案，旨在保护用户隐私，因为技术被用来跟踪新型冠状病毒的传播。
这项提案被称为“曝光通知隐私法”，旨在确保人们不会被强迫使用这项技术。它还将确保这些数据不会被用于广告或商业目的，并且人们可以删除他们的数据。该法案试图要求通知系统只依赖于来自医疗机构的授权诊断。
来自华盛顿的民主党参议员、该法案的发起人之一玛丽亚·坎特维尔(Maria Cantwell)在提供给CNET的评论中表示：公共卫生需要负责任何通知系统，因此我们保护人们的隐私，当有人警告他们可能接触到新冠肺炎时，我们会帮助他们知道。
坎特维尔法案的共同发起人是来自路易斯安那州的共和党参议员比尔·卡西迪(Bill Cassidy)。来自明尼苏达州的民主党人艾米·克洛布查尔(Amy Klobuchar)也给予了她支持。
谷歌正在与苹果一起研究联系人追踪技术，该公司拒绝置评。卡西迪和克洛布查尔的办公室没有回复记者的置评请求，苹果也没有。
这种冠状病毒会导致一种名为新冠肺炎的呼吸道疾病，去年年底在中国武汉市首次检测到这种病毒。从那时起，它已经成为一场全面的大流行，感染了超过620万人，导致全球超过37万人死亡。疫情已经导致城市和整个国家宣布封锁，关闭商店，取消活动，并迫使公民留在家里帮助控制冠状病毒。一些地点已经开始重新开放，但专家警告说，感染的风险仍然存在。
公司一直在研究加快接触者追踪过程的技术，这反过来又会减缓新冠肺炎的传播。接触者追踪，以普通的、老式的方式进行，是劳动密集型的，人们要追踪所有与疾病检测呈阳性的人有过接触的人。
苹果和谷歌在4月份表示，他们正在合作一项重大努力，以阻止新冠肺炎病毒的传播。该病毒使用人们手机发出的信号，警告他们，如果他们在过去14天里与检测出埃博拉病毒呈阳性的人有过联系。这项技术于5月下旬向政府卫生当局开放，阿拉巴马州、北达科他州和南卡罗来纳州是最早使用这项技术的州之一。
这个联合项目利用了世界上两个最受欢迎的移动操作系统--苹果的iOS7和谷歌的Android--潜在地接触到数十亿人。这些工具将使用蓝牙无线电技术来支持公共卫生当局将开发的应用程序，所有iPhone手机和搭载谷歌Android软件的设备将能够相互通信。
隐私权观察人士和公民自由倡导者警告称，依赖技术进行接触者追踪将在政府做出公共卫生决策时造成谁被计算在内的差异。苹果和谷歌试图通过调整系统的工作方式来缓解人们对隐私的担忧。首先，它将是选择加入的，这意味着它在默认情况下不会被打开。
公共知识政策顾问萨拉·柯林斯在一份声明中表示，我们需要对向新冠肺炎提供曝光通知的应用进行监管，以保护用户的隐私，防止数据滥用，并维护我们的公民权利--这项法案为这三项工作提供了路线图。该法案标志着在保护美国人数据的漫长道路上迈出了宝贵的第一步。

note_en:
A group of US senators on Monday introduced a bill to regulate contact-tracing apps, aiming to protect user  privacy as technology is used to track the spread of the novel   coronavirus.
 The proposal is called the  Exposure Notification Privacy Act and seeks to ensure that people couldn't be forced to use the technology. It also would make sure that the data isn't used for advertising or commercial purposes and that people can delete their data. The bill seeks to require that notification systems only rely on "an authorized diagnosis" that came from medical organization.
  "Public health needs to be in charge of any notification system so we protect people's privacy and help them know when there is a warning that they might have been exposed to COVID-19,"  Sen. Maria Cantwell, a Democrat from Washington and one of the bill's sponsors, said in a comment provided to CNET.
 Cantwell's co-sponsor on the bill is Sen. Bill Cassidy, a Republican from Louisiana. Amy Klobuchar, a Democrat from Minnesota, also has given her support.
 Google, which is working on technology for contact tracing along with  Apple, declined to comment. The offices of Cassidy and Klobuchar didn't respond to requests for comment, nor did Apple.
 The  coronavirus, which causes a respiratory illness called COVID-19, was first detected in the Chinese city of Wuhan late last year. Since that time, it's become a full-blown pandemic, infecting  over 6.2 million people and killing over 370,000 around the globe. The outbreak has caused cities and entire countries to issue lockdowns, shuttering stores,  canceling events and forcing citizens to stay at home to help contain the coronavirus. Some locations have started re-opening, but experts warn the risk of infection remains.
                                             Companies have been working on technology to speed up the contact tracing process, which in turn would slow the spread of COVID-19. Contact tracing, done the normal, old-school way, is labor intensive, with people tracking down everyone who's been in touch with someone who test positive for the disease.
 Apple and Google in April said they were  working together on a major effort to stop the spread of  COVID-19 that uses signals from people's  phones to warn them if they've been in contact with someone over the past 14 days who's tested positive for the disease. The technology   became available to government health authorities in late May, with Alabama, North Dakota and South Carolina among the first to use it.
 The joint project takes advantage of two of the world's most popular  operating systems -- Apple's  iOS and Google's Android -- to potentially reach billions of people. The tools will use Bluetooth radio technology to support apps that will be developed by public health authorities, and  iPhones and devices powered by Google's Android software  will be able to communicate with each other.
 Privacy watchers and civil liberties advocates have warned that relying on technology for contact tracing will create a disparity on who is counted when governments make public health decisions. Apple and Google have sought to alleviate fears about privacy by  tweaking how the system will work. For one, it will be opt-in, meaning it won't be turned on by default.
   "We need to regulate apps that provide COVID-19 exposure notification to protect a user's privacy, prevent data misuse and preserve our civil rights -- and this bill offers a roadmap for doing all three," Public Knowledge Policy Counsel Sara Collins said in a statement. "The bill marks a valuable first step in the long road ahead to protecting Americans' data."
  Update, 5:10 p.m. PT: Adds confirmation of bill being proposed, along with quotes.   Update, 12:55 p.m. PT on June  2: Adds Google declining to comment.

title: 参议员提出新冠肺炎联系人追踪隐私法案

路透旧金山8月23日电-Facebook(FB.O)首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)周二告诉员工，他坚持自己的决定，不挑战美国总统唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)发布的煽动性帖子，在员工罕见地举行公开抗议的一天后，他拒绝让步。
周一，一群Facebook员工-由于冠状病毒大流行，几乎所有人都在家里工作-举行了罢工。他们抱怨说，该公司应该针对特朗普的帖子采取行动，这些帖子包含这样一句话：“当抢劫开始时，枪击就开始了。”
扎克伯格的一位女发言人说，扎克伯格在视频聊天中告诉员工，Facebook已经进行了彻底的审查，让这些帖子不受质疑是正确的。
她说，扎克伯格也承认这一决定让许多员工感到不安，并表示公司正在寻找“非二元”的选择，而不是留下这样的职位或将他们撤下。
周一在推特上发表批评言论的一名Facebook员工在全体会议期间再次在Twitter上发帖，表达了失望之情。
Facebook员工布兰登·戴尔(Brandon Dail)在推特上写道：“今天很明显，领导层拒绝与我们站在一起。”戴尔在LinkedIn上的个人资料显示，他是西雅图Facebook的一名用户界面工程师。
周五，推特公司(TWTR.N)在特朗普的一条推文上贴上了警告标签，这条推文涉及明尼苏达州一名黑人男子死亡引发的广泛抗议活动，其中包括一句话：“当抢劫开始时，枪击就开始了。”
推特表示，这篇帖子违反了禁止美化暴力的规定，但被作为公共利益例外，减少了互动和分发的选择。
Facebook拒绝就同样的信息采取行动，扎克伯格试图将他的公司与总统和Twitter之间的斗争保持距离。他坚称，虽然他认为特朗普的言论“极具冒犯性”，但这些言论并没有违反公司反对煽动暴力的政策。
推特上周还在特朗普的两条推文上贴上了事实核查标签，其中包含关于邮寄选票的误导性说法。Facebook将政客的帖子排除在其第三方事实核查程序之外，并未对该帖子采取任何行动。
Facebook团队致力于打击虚假信息的初级软件工程师蒂莫西·阿韦尼(Timothy Aveni)宣布辞职，以抗议这一决定。
“马克总是告诉我们，他将与呼吁暴力的言论划清界限。他在周五向我们证明了这是个谎言。每次特朗普升级时，Facebook都会继续移动球门柱，寻找一个又一个借口不采取行动，“他在Facebook上写道。
周一晚间，民权领袖与扎克伯格和其他Facebook高管一起参加了长达一小时的视频通话，他们称这位首席执行官为特朗普不插手做法的辩护是“令人费解的”。
“他没有表现出对历史或现代选民压制的理解，他拒绝承认Facebook是如何为特朗普呼吁对抗议者采取暴力行动提供便利的，”公民与人权领导人会议、全国有色人种协进会(NAACP)法律辩护和教育基金以及变化之色(Color Of Change)的领导人发表了一份联合声明。
一些批评者在推特上呼吁Facebook的独立监督委员会参与进来。但在初秋之前，董事会将不会审查任何案件，用户最初只能就删除的内容向董事会提出上诉，而不是Facebook决定原封不动的内容。可以否决扎克伯格的董事会只会审查一小部分内容决定。
note_en:
SAN FRANCISCO (Reuters) - Facebook (  FB.O) CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees on Tuesday that he stood by his decision not to challenge inflammatory posts by U.S. President Donald Trump, refusing to give ground a day after staff members staged a rare public protest.
  A group of Facebook employees - nearly all of them working at home due to the coronavirus pandemic - walked off the job on Monday. They complained the company should have acted against Trump’s posts containing the phrase “when the looting starts, the shooting starts.”
 Zuckerberg told employees on a video chat that Facebook had conducted a thorough review and was right to leave the posts unchallenged, a company spokeswoman said.
 She said Zuckerberg also acknowledged the decision had upset many employees and said the company was looking into “non-binary” options beyond either leaving up such posts or taking them down.
 One Facebook employee, who tweeted criticism on Monday, posted again on Twitter during the all-hands meeting to express disappointment.
 “It’s crystal clear today that leadership refuses to stand with us,” Facebook employee Brandon Dail wrote on Twitter. Dail’s LinkedIn profile describes him as a user interface engineer at Facebook in Seattle.
 On Friday, Twitter Inc (  TWTR.N) affixed a warning label to a Trump tweet about widespread protests over the death of a black man in Minnesota that included the phrase “when the looting starts, the shooting starts.”
 Twitter said the post violated its rules against glorifying violence but was left up as a public interest exception, with reduced options for interactions and distribution.
 Facebook declined to act on the same message, and Zuckerberg sought to distance his company from the fight between the president and Twitter. He maintained that while he found Trump’s remarks “deeply offensive,” they did not violate company policy against incitements to violence.
 Twitter last week also put a fact-checking label on two Trump tweets containing misleading claims about mail-in ballots. Facebook, which exempts politicians’ posts from its program with third-party fact-checkers, took no action on that post.
 Timothy Aveni, a junior software engineer on Facebook’s team dedicated to fighting misinformation, announced his resignation in protest over that decision.
 “Mark always told us that he would draw the line at speech that calls for violence. He showed us on Friday that this was a lie. Facebook will keep moving the goalposts every time Trump escalates, finding excuse after excuse not to act,” he wrote in a Facebook post.
 Civil rights leaders who attended an hour-long video call on Monday night with Zuckerberg and other top Facebook executives called the CEO’s defense of the hands-off approach to Trump’s “incomprehensible.”
 “He did not demonstrate understanding of historic or modern-day voter suppression and he refuses to acknowledge how Facebook is facilitating Trump’s call for violence against protesters,” said a joint statement from leaders of The Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights, the NAACP Legal Defense and Educational Fund and Color of Change.
 Some critics posted calls on Twitter for Facebook’s independent oversight board to weigh in. But the board will not review any cases until early fall, and users initially will only be able to appeal to the board about removed content, not content that Facebook has decided to leave untouched. The board, which can overrule Zuckerberg, will only review a small slice of content decisions.
title: Facebook员工罢工后，扎克伯格为没有对特朗普的帖子采取行动进行辩护
title_en: After Facebook staff walkout, Zuckerberg defends no action on Trump posts

note:
朱利安·杰恩斯(Julian Jaynes)的“两院制精神崩溃中的意识起源”是一本精彩的书，只有两个小缺点。首先，它声称要解释意识的起源。第二，它假设了两院制思维的崩溃。我认为可以绕过这些缺陷，同时保持论文的原封不动。所以我将从评论一本稍有不同的书开始，这本书是杰恩斯应该写的。然后我会谈一谈他实际写的那本更可疑的书。
我假设的Jaynes2.0是一本关于心理理论的书。心理理论是我们思维如何运作的直观模型。它与关于大脑是如何由认知算法组成或如何在大脑中的神经元上实例化的智力理论无关。每个小学生都有心理理论。它是这样的：头脑是一个想象的空间，包含着思想、情感和欲望等东西。我有我的，你有你的。我能看到我脑子里的东西，但看不到你脑子里的东西，反之亦然。我主要选择在任何给定时间在我脑海中的事情：我会想着要发生，它们就会发生；我会自己做决定，然后它就会做出。这需要一种叫做意志力的资源，如果我没有足够的意志力，有时候我脑海中发生的事情并不是我想要的。当重要的事情发生时，有时我的大脑会产生强烈的情绪；这是很自然的，但我需要使用大量的意志力来确保我不会被它们淹没并做出糟糕的决定。
所有这一切似乎如此明显，以至于听起来像是常识，而不是理论。但这是必须要学会的。很小的孩子并不是从心理理论开始的。他们无法将自己从情绪中分离出来；他们说“我现在真的很生气，但这只是我感觉到的一件事，我并不是真的恨你”，这是不自然的。他们甚至不清楚人们的大脑包含着不同的东西；众所周知，孩子们无法理解一个拥有与他们不同证据的玩伴可能会得出不同的结论。
学习不只是被动地坐着观察你自己的思想，直到你弄清楚它是如何运作的。你是从你父母那里学来的。父母总是告诉他们的孩子“我这么想”和“你觉得呢？”还有“你看起来很悲伤”和“当你这么做的时候让我感到难过”。最终，这一切都被理解了。孩子们学习父母的心理理论，就像他们学习父母的语言或宗教一样。
在人类历史上，心理理论最早是在什么时候出现的？这不可能是一项单一的发明--更像是一个逐步完善的过程。“潜意识”只有在弗洛伊德的时候才真正进入我们的心理理论。像“我的虐待给了我很多包袱，我还在努力解决”这样的说法，涉及的心理理论在几个世纪前是无法理解的。这就像“我用鼠标点击一个图标”--每个单词都有意义，但是格式塔就是无稽之谈。
尽管如此，每个人都总是假设绝对的基础-心灵作为一个包含信仰和情感的隐喻空间，人们有思想和做出决定-必须追溯到如此之远，以至于他们的起源迷失在时间的迷雾中，只能归因于无名的猿人。
朱利安·杰恩斯可不这么想。他认为它来自公元前1500-750年的近东青铜时代。
杰恩斯(写于20世纪70年代)既是普林斯顿大学的心理学教授，也是古语言专家，因此是提出这一观点的完美人选。他回顾了这一时期前后的各种青铜时代的写作样本，发现早期的作品没有提到心理过程，而后期的作品提到了心理过程。当早期的著作确实提到了心理过程时，它们出现在学者们一致认为是后来的插入物的部分中。如果你在对语言本身一无所知的情况下，根据对心理过程的引用程度，试图找出经过大量编辑的古代文本中哪些部分是早期的，哪些是晚期的，你可以做得相当不错。
我不会说流利的苏美尔语，所以我不得不相信杰恩斯的话。更糟糕的是，因为杰恩斯认为，其他翻译者有时会犯错误，用精神的方式翻译非心理术语。这对他们来说是一个很容易犯的错误，因为大多数文化一旦获得了心理理论，就会重新调整现有语言的用途来代表它。杰恩斯提出了一个令人信服的理由来解释为什么会发生这种情况，并令人信服地论证了为什么他的解释更符合文本的精神，但这确实意味着你不能通过阅读翻译的作品来复查他的作品。
杰恩斯谈论伊利亚特的时间最多，这是有充分理由的-这是我们拥有的最长的青铜时代作品，从很多方面来说，它是一部心理剧，关注阿喀琉斯、赫克托等人物的程度与情节本身一样多。大约在公元前1100年到公元前800年，通过希腊吟游诗人的努力，它零散地结合在一起，最终在公元前700年左右达到了“荷马”口中的规范版本-杰恩斯说，这段时期心理理论开始演变。Jaynes用它来追踪发展过程，展示了“伊利亚特”的老章节是如何以不同于新章节的方式对待心理学的。
例如，典型的翻译可能会使用“恐惧充斥着阿伽门农的头脑”这样的短语。不对!。“伊利亚特”中没有“心”这个词，也许除了最新的插入词。这些词类似于卡迪亚(Kardia)、Noos、phrenes和拇指，Jaynes将它们分别翻译为心脏、视觉/知觉、腹部和交感神经系统。他可能会把关于阿伽门农的那句话翻译成“阿伽门农肚子里颤抖的玫瑰”之类的话。它的意思仍然是一样的--阿伽门农很害怕--但如果你不知道“头脑”是精神疾病发生的地方，你就会发现你的肚子颤抖得更厉害了，这就是你谈论这件事的方式。后来，当希腊人有了心理理论后，他们改变了所有这些术语的用途。今天，你仍然可以找到这样的迹象，比如我们怎么说“我心里相信它”。事实上，你仍然可以找到膈的这种分裂用法，在英语中，它既是膈神经(腹部的一种神经)，也是精神分裂症(一种精神疾病)。随着过渡的进行，人们对你的肚子、你的心或其他任何东西的感觉变得越来越花哨，直到最终肚子、心和所有其他东西融合成一个单一的头脑，所有的精神事物都发生在一起。
“伊利亚特”使用这些身体部位来描述感觉，尽管它的心理理论很薄弱。其用于描述思想和决策的解决方案更具…。不合常规。
假设阿喀琉斯怒不可遏，想要杀死阿伽门农。但这将是一个糟糕的[想法]；经过一段时间的[考虑]后，他[决定]反对。如果阿喀琉斯对括号中的任何一个词都没有概念，甚至没有任何与这些术语稍有对应的概念，他是如何对自己的行为进行概念化的呢？杰恩斯：
阿喀琉斯的反应开始于他的埃托尔，或者我建议的是他的肠子抽筋，在那里他处于冲突中，或者分成两个部分(Mermerizo)，是否服从他的拇指，愤怒的直接内在感觉，杀死国王，或者不服从他的大拇指，杀死国王，或者不服从他的拇指，直接内心的愤怒，杀死国王，或者不服从他的拇指。只有在阿喀琉斯拔出强大的剑时，腹部感觉和血液激增的摇摆不定的间歇之后，压力才足以让梦幻般的闪闪发光的女神雅典娜产生幻觉，然后她接管了动作的控制权，并告诉阿喀琉斯该怎么做。
当你开始一天的工作时，你会听到一个声音告诉你要做什么，赞扬你的成功，批评你的失败，并告诉你在困难的情况下应该做出什么决定。现代心理理论告诉你，这是你自己的声音，思考的想法。它如此始终如一地、令人信服地说出了这一点，以至于我们从未停下来质疑它是否可能是其他什么。
如果你没有心理理论，你会用它做什么？孩子们没有心理理论，至少不是很多，超过一半的孩子有想象中的朋友。Jaynes对想象中的朋友现象做了一些研究，他认为更好的说法应该是“幻觉朋友”--孩子们生动地看到和听到这些实体。没有理论的头脑是一种绝望的东西，它会遵从你给它的任何先例，以使它的经验变得有意义。如果你头脑中的声音是朋友或神，它会顺从地为你幻觉朋友或神，并相应地调整它的声音。
我认识一些非常聪明，在其他方面完全理智的福音派基督徒，他们向我发誓上帝会回应他们的祈祷。他们会问上帝一个问题，他们会听到上帝的声音来回答。上帝的声音可能听起来并不完全像外部声音，它可能只会给他们提供如果他们想过就会给自己的建议-但他们发誓他们没有考虑过这一点，他们的经历与此有质的不同。这些都是普通人！如果你是一个特殊的人--比如说圣人或神秘主义者--你通过禁食、祈祷、使你的身体承受最大压力来积极地追求这种体验，那么声音就会更响亮、更有说服力。
甚至有基于这种东西的各种形式的治疗。在“内部家庭系统”中，治疗师要求患者将他们大脑的某一部分(可能是产生某种症状的部分)概念化为一个人，并与之交谈。我知道有些人发誓这很管用。他们接近他们的悲伤、愤怒或焦虑，他们对“他”或“她”的样子有一个清晰的印象，然后“他”或“她”与他们交谈。通常他/她会告诉他们一些适当的心理上的事情，比如“你好，我是你的焦虑症，我之所以把这些恐惧强加给你，是因为我们小时候被虐待过，我想确保再也没有人像这样虐待我们了。”然后，患者谈到他们的焦虑，并希望达成协议，患者同意考虑焦虑的观点，而焦虑同意不让患者一直如此焦虑。一些人信誓旦旦地说，这对他们帮助很大，没有其他东西能帮到他们，并且绝对坚持他们是在与焦虑进行真正的对话，而不是虚构对话的双方。
大多数看起来很喜欢IFS的人都有边缘性人格障碍。处于边缘状态的人患分离性身份障碍(多重人格)的风险也最大。多重人格有两个主要的风险因素：一是边缘，二是有人向你建议多重人格障碍可能是合理的。在80年代的一段时间里，精神病学家真的很喜欢多重人格，并试图诊断每个人都有多重人格，果然所有这些人都会承认拥有多重人格，这将是非常令人兴奋的。然后，美国心理学会告诉精神病学家停止，人们不再那么多地谈论多重人格，现在这种情况更加罕见了。
几年前，有人重新发现/发明了tulpamancy，一种有目的地培养多种个性的想法，因为它很酷。试图做到这一点的人通常会成功。至少他们说他们成功了，我相信他们是这么想的。我认为他们的内在体验是与他们内心的另一个实体对话。此外，我有一个写小说的朋友，有一次她为她的一个人物创造了一个如此详细的心理模型，以至于它变成了另一种性格，她现在仍然拥有这种性格，并认为这是她生活中重要的一部分。她是我认识的最实际的人之一，通常不容易幻想。
我还有一些不太实际的朋友，那些迷恋神秘学的朋友。他们告诉我，他们有时会与灵性实体接触。当他们说他们有这些经历时，我相信他们。当他们说他们不是故意引导他们的占卜板说任何话时，我相信他们。我没有任何朋友足够酷，可以完成召唤你的神圣守护天使的整个过程，但从我读到的情况来看，直接完成仪式会给你留下一个天使，他会在你身边徘徊，给你提供建议。我相信那些说这是他们完成仪式的经历的人。
我的结论是，在合适的情况下，给自己赋予多种个性实际上是相当容易的。这些情况是一种糟糕的心理理论(我认为边界线在这方面天生就不好)，也是一种文化背景，在这种文化背景下，拥有多重人格是被期待的。
杰恩斯说，古代人满足这两个标准。他们完全没有心理理论，比起现在最小的孩子，他们的心理理论更少。他们的文化背景绝对确定神是存在的。就像我们教育我们的孩子，他们头脑中的声音是他们在思考自己一样，古人也会告诉他们的孩子，他们头脑中的声音是给他们发号施令的上帝。声音会尽职尽责地塑造自己，以适应预期的角色。
在这里，杰恩斯是他最聪明的，逐一阅读古代文本，注意到完全缺乏精神意象，并突出了许多日常与神对话的例子。每一种古老的文化都有几乎相同的神的概念，神坐在你的身体里，告诉你该做什么。希腊人有他们的精灵，罗马人有他们的精灵，埃及人有他们的ka和ba，美索不达米亚人有他们。
打开特里·普拉切特(Terry Pratchett)所说的“一见钟情”，试着用全新的眼光看待青铜时代。真的很奇怪。人们会将他们的城市围绕着一个巨大的Ziggurat，“上帝之家”，里面有一个巨大的偶像。他们会像对待活人一样对待这个偶像--每天喂它，每天洗它，有时甚至让它坐着奴隶队伍抬着的轿子在街上游行，这样它就可以去异性偶像的寺庙进行一次“夫妻式探望”！国王死后，成百上千的人会努力为他建造一座巨大的坟墓，然后他们会杀掉一群人，让他们在来世为他服务。有时，一切都会崩溃，每个人都会偷偷溜进山里，试图假装他们没有在过去20年里建造一座珠光宝气的方尖碑，这样一个叫宁阿尔-伊迪达的人就可以吹嘘他有多少奴隶。
朱利安·杰恩斯认为，如果青铜时代看起来有点像蜂群思维，那是因为它的居民并不是完全独立的，至少不是我们所认为的个性。他们像精神分裂症患者一样处于恍惚状态，听着命令他烧毁医院的声音。所有这一切--之字塔、方尖碑、金字塔--不是为了捕捉人类个体，而是为了捕捉那些人类的精灵--让人们将脑海中的声音与当地的神联系起来，并用一个幻觉的神取代他们的自由意志，这个神代表着他们对社会要求的精神模式。“。在最好的情况下，声音会被解释为神王本人，从几英里外给你发号施令。杰恩斯辩称，青铜时代痴迷于埋葬和来世(例如金字塔)，因为如果你将脑海中的声音内化为法老乔普斯，这个声音不会因为真正的法老乔普斯死在数百英里外的首都而消失。因此，即使在法老乔普斯死后，据他所有的臣民所知，他仍然在附近，从来世指挥他们。所以他们最好让他真的，真的快乐，就像他们在生活中所做的那样。Jaynes提出了各种证据，证明金字塔的主要功能是作为一个你可以与死去的法老的灵魂交流的地方--即问它问题，它会回答你的问题。
他对偶像也有类似的解释。青铜时代热爱偶像。那里有巨大的神像，这些神像使奥林匹亚的宙斯雕像看起来像是一件软弱的作品。而且，每个家庭都有自己的个人偶像。挖掘青铜时代房屋的考古学家只是发现了一个又一个偶像，就像古代苏美尔人除了整天盯着偶像什么都不做一样。杰恩斯认为这大致是真的。偶像要么是催生幻觉声音的线索，要么只是让谈话变得更舒适--毕竟，如果你能看到你正在交谈的人，那就不那么令人毛骨悚然了。
然后，大约在公元前1250年，这个运转良好的系统开始崩溃。杰恩斯将此归咎于贸易。商人总是带着不同的神进入其他国家。这些新的国家将会令人困惑，交易员的幻觉声音不会总是知道所有的答案。然后，他们将不得不与竞争对手商家谈判！在这里，心理理论成为一个巨大的优势-你需要能够模拟你的竞争对手的想法，以便从他那里获得最好的交易。你的对手也想要心理理论，这样他就能想出如何欺骗你。大约在公元前1250年，贸易开始回升，这些考虑变成了更大的问题。然后大约在公元前1200年，青铜时代崩溃了。原因还不是很清楚(你们中的一些人可能听过我关于这一点的陈述)，尽管大多数猜测涉及气候变化和神秘的海洋生物。整个帝国都被摧毁了，他们的人民成了难民，依次涌入下一个帝国。现在每个人都在陌生的领域，没有人知道所有的答案。几个交易员养成的奇怪的思维习惯变得至关重要；人们要么收养它们，要么死亡。
但是随着心智理论的传播，众神的声音逐渐消失。他们从固定的同伴，到只在压力大的时候出现(最重要的决定)，甚至从来没有出现过。杰恩斯基本上将公元前1000年至公元前700年之间发生的一切解释为越来越疯狂地试图将神带回来或处理一个没有神的世界。
现在，为了公平起见，他引用了这个时代的大约一千亿篇文学作品，主题是“神已经抛弃了我们”和“刚刚发生了什么，为什么神已经不存在了？”像往常一样，其他人都软弱无力，用比喻的方式解释这些-声称这只是美索不达米亚人表达他们在这个混乱时期感到多么不幸的一种诗意方式。杰恩斯认为这不是一个比喻--首先，人们永远都不走运，但是公元前1000-750年
Jaynes说，“在我在这里描述的文本之前的任何文学作品中，都没有任何这样的担忧的痕迹”。
所以人们变得绝望了。他说，这一时期是占卜和占卜的起源。在此之前，征兆“可能以微不足道的方式出现”，但不是。
